customer_anxiety_profile: 73-year-old cash buyer with a life-stage ROI concern ('if I gotta put a bunch of money in it, it wasn't worth it to me'). Not a financing objection — Dave pays cash for everything including cars. His anxiety is about whether the investment makes sense at his age and timeline. He also rejected the in-home visit format and accepted only a phone call, signaling some discomfort with in-person sales pressure.
unresolved_concerns: ROI / payback timeline at age 73 — never addressed, only deflected with financing framing
Actual system cost — Dave asked three times and never got a direct answer
Whether the program makes financial sense for a cash buyer specifically
sensitive_topics_raised: Age and life-stage ROI ('I'm 73 years old')
exact_promises_rep_made: Phone call tomorrow at 12PM (not an in-home visit)
Specialist will bring the design and answer all questions
Dave should not be driving and should have access to email to view photos during the call
closer_should_know: Dave is a cash buyer who explicitly rejected financing framing — lead with the purchase option and the payback math for his specific usage (929 kWh/month on the main meter), not the bill-swap/no-upfront-cost pitch. His son is on the title and was not included in the appointment; if the son is a decision participant, the closer should surface that early. The pool house (separate meter, ~$30/month) was mentioned but dismissed by Dave — flag as a potential secondary structure cross-sell if the conversation opens up.
